name: In this connection, too, there was a misapprehension at first. Some thought that eating
introduction:
and drinking were absolutely prohibited after the performance of the 'Isha' (Night) Prayer.
Others thought that one could eat and drink so long as one had not fallen asleep, but that if
one had it was not permissible to eat on reawakening. These were people's own fancies and
often caused great inconvenience. This verse seeks to remove all such misconceptions. It
clearly lays down the duration of the fast: from dawn until sunset. Between sunset and
dawn it is permissible to eat, to drink, and to indulge in the legitimate gratification of sexual
desires.
At the same time the Prophet introduced the pre-fasting repast, recommending a good meal
just before dawn.
